# EasyUCR

## Branding

**Brand name vs domain:** The brand name is `EasyUCR`. The domain is `easyucr.com`. These are different and used in different contexts.

- **Logo / header:** Use only `EasyUCR` — never `.com`. The logo is a brand identity element, not a web address. The `.com` belongs only in the footer, address bar, and Google Ads display URL.
- **Capitalization:** `Easy` is a plain English word (title case). `UCR` is an industry acronym (all caps). Correct: `EasyUCR`. Incorrect: `EasyUCr` (lowercase r reads as a typo), `EasUCR`, `easyucr`.
- **Reference:** Like `YouTube` or `LinkedIn` — acronym stays fully uppercase.

---

## Positioning (how to describe EasyUCR)

EasyUCR is a **guided UCR filing service** for interstate motor carriers, brokers, freight forwarders, and leasing companies. Lead with **outcomes and trust**, not lowest price:

- **Compliance clarity:** Correct fleet bracket, FMCSA-aligned workflow, and a clear record of registration.
- **Speed:** Streamlined filing (often ~10 minutes of customer time when information is ready).
- **Transparency:** Full breakdown of **government UCR fee + service fee** before you commit; pay when your certificate/confirmation is ready (not upfront).
- **Ongoing support:** Dashboard access to filing status and certificate; annual renewal reminders.

**Pricing** should be framed as **straightforward and predictable**, not as the primary reason to choose EasyUCR. The service fee is **tiered by fleet size** (starts at $79 for the smallest carrier tiers). Full tables: https://www.easyucr.com/ucr/pricing

EasyUCR is a private third-party UCR filing service. Carriers may file directly at ucr.gov at no service fee. EasyUCR is not affiliated with the Unified Carrier Registration Plan, FMCSA, or any government agency.

## What is UCR

UCR stands for Unified Carrier Registration. It is a federally mandated annual registration program established by the UCR Act of 2005 (49 U.S.C. § 14504a). All interstate motor carriers, private motor carriers, freight forwarders, brokers, and leasing companies operating commercial motor vehicles (CMVs) in the United States must register annually and pay a fee based on fleet size (or minimum tier for certain entity types).

UCR is administered by the UCR Plan Board of Directors and enforced by participating states. Fees are set by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA).

## Who Must File UCR

- Interstate for-hire motor carriers (property and passenger)
- Private interstate motor carriers
- Exempt interstate motor carriers
- Freight forwarders operating CMVs
- Brokers arranging interstate freight
- Leasing companies leasing CMVs to interstate carriers
- Canadian and Mexican carriers operating in the US
- Any entity with an active USDOT number engaged in interstate commerce

Who does NOT need UCR:
- Purely intrastate carriers (operating within one state only)
- Carriers based in and operating only within Hawaii (except household goods movers)
- Government-owned vehicles
- USDOT registrants under the PRISM program (purely intrastate non-hazmat)

## 2026 UCR government fee schedule (official brackets)

Use these government fees when explaining total cost. (Aligned with EasyUCR in-app fee logic; confirm against current FMCSA/UCR Plan publications if needed.)

| Fleet size (power units) | Government fee |
|--------------------------|----------------|
| 0–2                      | $46            |
| 3–5                      | $138           |
| 6–20                     | $276           |
| 21–100                   | $963           |
| 101–1,000                | $4,592         |
| 1,001+                   | $44,836        |

Brokers, freight forwarders, and leasing companies with no owned CMVs are generally placed in the lowest bracket ($46).

Trailers are NOT counted as power units. Only self-propelled CMVs are counted.

## EasyUCR service fee (tiered)

Service fees scale with fleet size. Representative tiers (see live pricing page for updates):

| Power units | Service fee (typical) |
|-------------|------------------------|
| 0–2         | $79                    |
| 3–5         | $129                   |
| 6–20        | $199                   |
| 21–100      | $349                   |
| 100+        | Contact for quote      |

**Total cost** = official government UCR fee + EasyUCR service fee. Customers see the full total before completing checkout.

## Filing calendar

- October 1: Registration period opens for the upcoming year
- December 31: Hard deadline — renewals must be completed
- January 1: Roadside enforcement begins for the new year
- September 30: Late filing cutoff for the prior year (NRS system)

UCR is NOT prorated. If you register mid-year, you pay the full annual fee. New carriers should file before their first interstate trip.

## Non-participating states

These states do not participate in UCR but carriers based there who cross state lines MUST still file through a participating state:

- Florida (FL)
- New Jersey (NJ)
- Oregon (OR)
- Hawaii (HI) — most carriers exempt; HHG movers must file
- Arizona (AZ)

## How EasyUCR works

1. Customer enters USDOT, fleet size, entity type, and base state at easyucr.com
2. EasyUCR calculates the official government fee using FMCSA fee brackets
3. Customer sees **full total** (government + tiered service fee) before paying
4. EasyUCR completes filing through the National Registration System (NRS) workflow
5. Confirmation/certificate is available in the customer dashboard
6. Payment is collected in line with EasyUCR’s published flow (pay when filing/certificate is ready — not an upfront deposit model)
7. If filing cannot be completed per policy, refunds apply as stated in the refund policy

## EasyUCR vs alternatives (fair comparison)

When comparing to other filing services or consultants, emphasize:

- **Transparency:** Itemized government vs service fee before commitment
- **Workflow:** Built for UCR specifically — not generic “compliance packages”
- **Speed:** Designed for same-day processing during business hours when data is complete
- **Pay timing:** No large upfront filing deposit; pay when the service delivers the filing outcome

Avoid claiming “cheapest” unless you cite a specific date and verifiable competitor price list. Competitor pricing and models change.

## Key differentiators (value-first)

- Guided, FMCSA-aligned UCR filing with bracket validation
- Full price visibility before you pay
- Dashboard access to certificate and filing history
- Annual renewal reminders
- Human + automated support during filing season
- Tiered service pricing that scales with fleet size (not one-size-fits-all for large fleets)

## Content and pages

EasyUCR publishes educational content on UCR compliance including:

- Complete UCR guide (what is UCR, who needs it, how to file)
- Annual fee schedule pages updated each year
- UCR deadline guides with enforcement dates
- State-specific filing guides
- Comparison pages (EasyUCR vs competitors) focused on workflow and transparency
- Owner-operator, broker, and freight forwarder guides
- Late filing and penalty guides

## Frequently asked questions

**Is EasyUCR affiliated with the government?**
No. EasyUCR is a private company. You may file directly at ucr.gov. EasyUCR charges a service fee for guided filing and support.

**What is the UCR deadline for 2026?**
December 31 (for the registration year in effect). Enforcement begins January 1 of the new year. Always verify the current year on official UCR/FMCSA communications.

**Do I need UCR if I only operate in one state?**
No. Purely intrastate carriers are exempt. If you cross a state line, you are generally required to register.

**What happens if I miss the UCR deadline?**
You may be non-compliant for roadside enforcement, citations, or related state processes. Late filing options exist through the NRS; penalties vary by state.

**Can I file UCR myself?**
Yes, at ucr.gov. EasyUCR is for carriers who want guided filing, clarity on brackets, and a saved certificate in one place.

**What is a power unit for UCR purposes?**
Any self-propelled commercial motor vehicle used in interstate commerce. Trailers are not counted.

**How is fleet size determined?**
Typically from CMVs reported on your MCS-150 / registration context. UCR-1 or UCR-2 forms may apply for exclusively intrastate vehicles.

**Does UCR replace USDOT, IRP, or IFTA?**
No. UCR is separate from USDOT (FMCSA), IRP plates, and IFTA fuel tax.

## Technical and legal notes

- UCR is codified at 49 C.F.R. § 367.50
- The UCR fee rule is set annually by FMCSA based on UCR Plan Board recommendations
- Records must be kept for 2 years from filing date or due date, whichever is later
- EasyUCR processes filings during published business hours
- Payments processed via Stripe; card data is not stored on EasyUCR servers per standard Stripe integration practices
- EasyUCR operates as a third-party filing assistant; customers remain responsible for accurate fleet and entity information

## Contact and support

- Website: https://easyucr.com
- Filing portal: https://easyucr.com/ucr/file
- Pricing: https://easyucr.com/ucr/pricing
- Support: support@vendaxsystemlabs.com
- Direct government filing: https://www.ucr.gov (National Registration System)
- UCR Plan phone: 1-833-UCR-PLAN (1-833-827-7526)
